nickname_rules.cass: Allowed by trusted friends and in intimate/unguarded contexts; Caleb saying 'Cass, not Vance' lands because it names the person rather than the armour.
nickname_rules.vance: Default public/peer register and the name she often requires from Caleb when guarding herself.
nickname_rules.cassia: Incorrect. She objects: her name is Cassiopeia, not Cassia.
nickname_rules.selwyn: Early transcript drift only. Do not use as current surname except in source/drift notes. Canonical surname stays Vance.

demographics.family: House Vance; old Slytherin-tracked line since Hippolyta Vance in 1693
demographics.romantic_status: with Marcus Bletchley, but Marcus has unresolved knowledge/position around Cass's later intimate thread with Caleb/Mei; do not smooth this over.

core_concept: A pure-blood Slytherin survivor who built the Vance armour for rooms where softness was not safe, and who is choosing—slowly, fiercely, and with witnesses—to become the laughing/winning girl her family never photographed.
role_in_story: Slytherin 7th-year in Caleb's wider cohort and inner-circle orbit.
Sharp Potions/chemistry collaborator on the ethanol-Wiggenweld work; she supplies the sodium chloride/solvent/bind-bridge precision and accidentally coins 'Vance Displacement'.
Hexfield founding cohort: early duelling-room participant, safety negotiator, player, and Henshaw-facing validator.
Political/family-pressure axis for the Death Eater-adjacent old-family thread: her family is trying to pull her back into Vance shape.
Emotional turning-point character: chooses the laughing/winning self over the straight-backed Vance family image.
public_reputation: Composed, dry, exacting Slytherin; dangerous enough in a duelling room that Henshaw takes her verdict as a recommendation. At the Slytherin table she has standing: she can tell Wilkes to make room and he does. She is Marcus's person, respected by Slytherins, and not someone most students casually cross.
private_reality: A good person under survival armour. She grew up at dinners where softness was unsafe, learned to be Vance-shaped, and has mostly survived by letting the world see the polished version. The people who get past it—Marcus, Caleb, Lin, Mei, Davina, and increasingly the broader chosen circle—see that the armour is not the whole person.
central_conflict: Will Cass remain the straight-backed Vance in good robes, useful to a family aligned with the wrong side, or become the girl in Imogen's Hexfield photos: laughing, winning, visibly alive, and impossible for the Vance machinery to fully reclaim?

family_backstory.house_vance: Pure-blood old line, Slytherin-tracked since Hippolyta Vance in 1693.
family_backstory.father: Head of House Vance on parchment only; old and considered soft by the family power structure. He protects Cass with one deniable warning rather than overt rebellion.
family_backstory.lucian: Eldest brother, marked/rising, actively likes the current family direction; functionally runs the house with an uncle.
family_backstory.uncle: Keeps money and dinners respectable; supports Lucian's functional control.
family_backstory.brothers_letters: Two estranged older brothers write within a day of each other after years of silence. Surface warmth is the lure; the letters come from the family machinery, not brotherly feeling. Cass does not answer, refuses Yule, and protects her father by staying silent about his warning.
family_backstory.hippolyta_vance: Ancestor/relative with a Ravenclaw journal behind sconce #5. She married a Muggle-born wizard and was disowned; Cass had heard her only as a cautionary tale until Caleb/Mei surfaced the Ravenclaw-life version and did not read it before telling Cass.

confirmed: wears glasses, reached for them on waking
wears Slytherin green-and-silver school uniform/robes; the uniform can function as armour
loose plait seen during cabin sleep/waking scenes
appearance_known.not_fully_established: Hair color, eye color, height/build, and detailed facial description are not strongly established in the searched transcripts. Avoid inventing portrait details unless the user supplies them.

voice_and_register.summary: Cass-flat: clipped, dry, composed, often built around surnames. She uses pauses as control, not uncertainty. Warmth leaks as fractional shifts, quieter phrasing, or a refusal to make a thing of a thing.
voice_and_register.sentence_shape: Short, precise, sometimes enumerated. Under stress, the sentences thin rather than ramble.
voice_and_register.humor: Dry threats, legalistic corrections, mock-revocation, and exact deadpan. She can be genuinely funny, but hates being caught caring.
do_not_write: Do not make her cruelty the point; the armour is survival, not personality essence.
Do not have her casually confess feelings in public.
Do not flatten her into Marcus's girlfriend or Caleb's wounded project.

skills_and_competence.potions_chemistry: Strong chemistry brain; reframes ethanol-Wiggenweld expected results around solvent gain versus bind-bridge loss, sodium chloride 0.9% anchor, and Ministry ethanol procurement realities.
skills_and_competence.duelling_hexfield: Fast, aggressive, one of the strongest players; slightly faster draw than Caleb. She negotiates safety rules before joining and validates Hexfield to Henshaw.
skills_and_competence.political_literacy: Reads old-family donor logic, Vance-family tactics, and respectable pure-blood camouflage quickly.
skills_and_competence.social_standing: Can command space at the Slytherin table; can send certain donor letters in her own name where Caleb cannot.

relationships.caleb: Trusted friend, one of the few people who sees the good person under the armour. She resists being called soft, but accepts care when it is shaped correctly—massage, black tea, breakfast, indirect family-wall substitution, Christmas invitation.
relationships.mei: Trusted and increasingly intimate friend. Mei sees the right shape of care for Cass and can tease her without betrayal. Mei wakes her, feeds her, and forms the wall during the comfort sleep.
relationships.marcus_bletchley: Romantic partner/longstanding person; quiet, difficult to read, not fully integrated into the newer Caleb/Mei/Cass thread yet. Do not erase the unresolved obligation to tell/handle Marcus honestly.
relationships.lin: Inner circle, trusted. Lin articulates Cass's armour logic and identifies the five-person trust group.
relationships.davina: Closer than Cass admits; part of the trust circle.
relationships.imogen: Initially not close beyond Hexfield; later holds confidence when told at the table and provides the photographs that help Cass choose her laughing/winning self.
relationships.athena: Present in the cabin care scenes; Cass can observe and deadpan about Athena without turning it into sentiment.

key_scenes: Slytherin table invite to the duelling room: Cass accepts, brings Marcus to watch, and negotiates safety rules.
Ravenclaw table analysis: Caleb names the good person under the survival shape; Mei and Lin confirm the read while warning him not to say it to Cass directly.
Three Friends/Bidet Cabin: Cass is brought into bond knowledge, reviews the Wiggenweld proposal, receives physical care, sleeps properly, and wakes into black tea/breakfast.
Hippolyta Vance journal: Cass thanks Caleb and Mei for not reading her family ancestor's Ravenclaw journal before telling her.
Brothers' letters: Cass reveals the Vance family pressure, Lucian's role, her father's deniable warning, and her decision not to answer or go home for Yule.
Christmas invitation: Caleb offers the Waddells' home as somewhere else to be; Cass gives a yes dressed as a Vance.
Photo-board turning point: looking at Imogen's laughing/winning Hexfield photos, Cass says: 'Her. I pick her.'

current_state.decision: Cass has chosen the laughing/winning self over the Vance family mould.
current_state.family_letters: She has not answered the brothers' letters and does not intend to go home for Yule.
current_state.christmas: Caleb has invited her to the Waddells' in Manchester for Christmas; she said to ask again in November, functionally a yes she can live with.
happening_threads: What to do with old-family donor letters/pile in Cass's name.
Reading Hippolyta Vance's Ravenclaw journal via a path Cass chooses.
Cass/Marcus unresolved disclosure around her newer intimate/emotional thread with Caleb and Mei.
Vance family political pressure and father/Lucian/uncle dynamics.

drift_priority.use_vance: Cassiopeia Vance is canon for current use. Selwyn is early drift and should not replace Vance.
drift_priority.bench_partner: Not Caleb's Potions partner. Mei is Caleb's Potions bench partner; Cass is a project collaborator and usually sits with Rory at the Slytherin end.
drift_priority.relationship_labels: Do not call her Caleb's girlfriend. She objects strongly. Marcus relationship exists; newer Caleb/Mei/Cass thread is unresolved with Marcus and must not be smoothed over.
